items.title: PTPTN announces 4.10pct dividend, highest in 11 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Depositors of the National Education Savings Scheme (Simpan SSPN) will receive the highest dividend in 11 years following the National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN)'s announcement of a 4.10 per cent dividend rate. Its chairman, Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim, said the 2025 dividend distribution involves an estimated allocation of RM505.68 million, benefiting 6.07 million depositor accounts. "The higher dividend rate reflects PTPTN's efficient fund management, supported by strong governance, integrity and strategic planning to ensure optimal returns for depositors," Norliza said at the Simpan SSPN 2025 dividend announcement ceremony here today.

items.title: Bangladeshi worker jailed six months, fined RM15,100 for molesting five women
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Bangladeshi construction worker who molested five women aged between 21 and 28 at MRT and LRT stations last month was jailed six months and fined RM15,100 by four magistrate's courts today. Before Aina Azahra, Romjan was jailed three weeks and fined RM3,500 after admitting to molesting a 21-year-old German woman at 12.40pm along the pedestrian walkway near the LRT Chan Sow Lin station on April 11. He was also fined RM3,600 after admitting to molesting a 24-year-old woman at an escalator of Maluri MRT station at 4.45pm on April 25.

items.title: How an ocean cruise turned into a hantavirus nightmare
items.summary: THE first victim of a suspected hantavirus outbreak had already been dead 21 days when fellow passenger Jake Rosmarin posted a video about cows he had seen on a remote volcanic island in the Atlantic, showing no indication he was aware his cruise ship was about to be quarantined. Three people on board - a Dutch couple and a German national - have died, the operator said. The first stricken passenger, the Dutch man, died on April 11, as the ship steamed towards Tristan da Cunha.

items.title: Flash floods hit parts of Klang Valley after heavy rain (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Heavy rain since 4pm today has triggered flash floods in several areas across the Klang Valley, disrupting traffic flow. A food court along Jalan 51a/223, Section 51A in Petaling Jaya inundated with flood water. Baniir kilat di Jalan 223, Petaling Jaya, Selangor.

items.title: Kota Belud faces 4.4-million‑litre daily water shortfall, assemblyman warns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 6 — A growing water supply deficit in Kota Belud, now short by about 4.4 million litres daily, emerged as the key concern raised by Tempasuk Assemblyman Datuk Mohd Arsad Bistari. He told the State Assembly that the district’s three water treatment plants — Kota Belud town, Bayayat and Pandasan — currently produce about 43 million litres per day against a demand of about 47.4 million litres. He urged immediate plans to boost supply including studies for mini dams at Babagon river and Takutan Rosok river.

items.title: Johor to set up special committee to review school safety following fatal accident
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor state government will set up a special committee to review school safety following an accident in Batu Pahat yesterday, where an 8-year-old girl was killed after being hit by a van outside her school. State Education and Information Committee chairman Aznan Tamin said that, apart from investigating the cause of the incident, the special committee will also focus on improving existing safety guidelines and coordinating safety measures at the state level. "The committee will also support the implementation of the Safe School Campaign, which covers not only road safety but also student safety within school grounds," he told reporters after attending the State Legislative Assembly sitting at the Sultan Ismail Building here today.

items.title: Second Malaysian Haj pilgrim dies in Mecca, 70-year-old Johor man succumbs to heart attack
items.summary: MECCA, May 6 — A 70-year-old male pilgrim died of a heart attack yesterday. Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) said in a statement today that the septugenarian, from Senggarang, Johor, died at Ajyad Hospital at 6.16pm local time (11.16pm Malaysian time). “The deceased was confirmed to have died of a heart attack while undergoing treatment at the hospital,” the statement said.

items.title: Pickpocket gang crippled, five members arrested in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA: The activities of a pickpocket syndicate targeting victims at tourist hotspots in the state were crippled when five of its members were arrested last week. Melaka Tengah district police chief Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the Chinese citizens, aged between 29 and 62, were believed to have operated in a group before targeting victims in crowded areas such as Jonker Walk. Christopher added that during the raids, the police seized various items believed to be the proceeds of criminal activities, including seven mobile phones of various brands, two sling bags, two wallets, foreign identification cards, and various foreign currencies.

items.title: UN demands Israel 'immediately' release Gaza flotilla activists
items.summary: GENEVA: The United Nations on Wednesday called on Israel to immediately release two activists taken from a Gaza-bound flotilla, and demanded an investigation into "disturbing accounts" they had been severely mistreated. Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avila, who are held in a prison in Ashkelon, were among dozens of activists on a Gaza-bound flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters off the coast of Greece last Thursday. "Israel must immediately and unconditionally release Global Sumud Flotilla members Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Avila, who were detained in international waters and brought to Israel where they continue to be held without charge," UN rights office spokesman Thameen Al-Kheetan said in a statement.

items.title: Ministry raises entry barriers on CBU EVs, ends special exemption
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry has tightened the requirements for fully imported (CBU) electric vehicles (EV) following the end of the special exemption for such vehicles. "Following the end of this exemption period, the policy related to EV CBU has reverted to the existing regulations. "However, taking into account that companies still have remaining stock, including existing stock, stock at ports, and stock in transit, the ministry allows such stock to be sold in accordance with the provisions under the special exemption period until it is fully depleted," it said in a statement.

items.title: Works Ministry rolls out EV charging system guidelines, first of its kind in Malaysia, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 6 — The Ministry of Works (KKR) launched the Design and Installation Guidelines for Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging Systems in conjunction with the 2026 Senior Officers’ Conference (SOC) of the Public Works Department (JKR). “This initiative aims to accelerate the development of EV charging infrastructure, not only at Rest and Service (R&R) areas along highways but also to be expanded to federal, state and municipal roads under JKR’s supervision,” he said to reporters here today. File picture of an electric vehicle (EV) charging at an EV charging station in Putrajaya June 18, 2024.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia closes higher on easing geopolitical tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today as easing West Asia tensions lifted overall market sentiment and encouraged a broader risk-on tone across regional equities, said an analyst. At 5 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 9.44 points, or 0.54 per cent, to 1,756.87 from Tuesday's close of 1,747.43. Turnover expanded to 3.98 billion units worth RM3.97 billion compared with 2.86 billion units worth RM2.59 billion yesterday.

items.title: Flash floods inundate food court in Petaling Jaya
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Heavy rain this afternoon triggered flash floods in an area in Section 51A here, submerging several vehicles in a parking area near a food court. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 4.15pm regarding flooding at a food court along Jalan 51A/223. He added that several vehicles were found partially submerged in the food court's parking area when firefighters arrived.

items.title: Fresh UAE attacks blamed on Iran draw new reality in the Gulf
items.summary: HOURS after the UAE's top oil executive said the country had "emerged stronger" from war and as thousands attended an Abu Dhabi summit to boost the local economy, phones across the country were alerting that missiles were incoming. The new attacks on Monday, which the UAE blamed on Iran, pushed schools to revert to remote learning, having resumed in-person classes roughly two weeks earlier. Iran targeted the UAE more than any other country during the war, hitting US assets but also energy and civilian infrastructure and even landmarks.

items.title: RM200 padi aid for farmers needs better targeting, govt told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Experts have called for better targeting of the RM200 per hectare aid for padi farmers, saying the support is relatively modest as production costs continue to rise. "The assistance is only meaningful if it helps farmers continue planting despite rising costs. She added that government support remains necessary as rice prices are regulated to contain inflation, limiting farmers' ability to pass on higher costs.

items.title: Two men charged with trafficking 130kg meth in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU, May 6 — Two men were charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with trafficking in 130.57 kilogrammes of methamphetamine into the country last month. According to the charge sheet, the two men were jointly charged with trafficking 130,575.18 grammes of Methamphetamine at about 9.30 pm at Batu 15 Jalan Apas here on April 21. The court set June 2 for mention.

items.title: Fahmi: Aminuddin remains Negeri Sembilan MB after Umno, Pakatan leaders resolve dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today the dispute surrounding the Negeri Sembilan government has been resolved following a meeting between the top leadership of Umno and Pakatan Harapan yesterday. Fahmi said the state government would remain under the leadership of Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. The Negeri Sembilan political crisis began after 14 Umno assemblymen from BN, led by state Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias, withdrew support for Aminuddin as menteri besar.

items.title: In Kedah, man to be charged tomorrow with grievous hurt after alleged assault on pregnant wife causes miscarriage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — A man accused of assaulting his pregnant wife, causing her to miscarry last month, will be charged at the Sungai Petani Magistrates’ Court tomorrow. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan confirmed the matter today, according to a report published by Harian Metro . The victim, a civil servant, is still receiving treatment at Hospital Sultan Abdul Halim in Sungai Petani.

items.title: High Court rejects influencer Ratu Naga’s application to refer legal questions to Federal Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — The High Court today dismissed Syarul Ema Rena Abu Samah’s bid to refer two questions of law to the Federal Court over being denied legal representation during questioning by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). Therefore, the court ruled that Syarul Ema Rena’s application did not meet the threshold under Section 84 of the Courts of Judicature Act 1964. On Jan 14, Syarul Ema Rena filed an application to refer two questions of law under Section 84 of the Courts of Judicature Act 1964.

items.title: Evacuations 'ongoing' from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.summary: PRAIA, Cape Verde: Evacuations were taking place Wednesday from a cruise ship stricken with a deadly outbreak of hantavirus, the World Health Organisation said, as experts confirmed a rare strain that can be transmitted between humans. Three people – two crew members and one other person – thought to be infected with the virus were being taken off the MV Hondius, anchored off Cape Verde, the WHO said. The ship's Dutch operator Oceanwide Expeditions had said on Tuesday that two seriously ill crew members – one British, one Dutch – and a passenger would be taken off the ship and flown to the Netherlands, allowing the vessel to sail on to Spain's Canary Islands.

items.title: Rafizi says Arm Holdings deal probe focuses on joint venture procedures, not money flows
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — Former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said investigations into him have so far centred on procedural matters rather than financial transactions in relation to the RM1.1 billion investment linked to a semiconductor deal between the Ministry of Economy and a UK-based company, Arm Holdings. Speaking to reporters outside the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today, he said no questions during his statement recording touched on money flows or payments, contrary to earlier claims. “There were no questions on financial transactions.

items.title: Cambodia says strife in Southeast Asia could crimp energy crisis response
items.summary: BANGKOK: Southeast Asia's joint effort to tackle global issues such as an energy crisis caused by the Iran war is complicated by internal friction over tension between Thailand and Cambodia and civil war in Myanmar, a senior Cambodian diplomat said on Wednesday. "Asean needs to put our house in order and sort things out as soon as possible," Cambodia's envoy to the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, Kung Phoak, told Reuters ahead of a summit of the bloc in the Philippines this week. Asean's unity is challenged by the grinding civil war raging in Myanmar since a 2021 coup, and tension still simmering between Thailand and Cambodia despite a December ceasefire in their deadly border war.

items.title: Operator of unregistered children’s care home pleads not guilty to carnal intercourse charges involving 15‑year‑old boy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — An operator of an unregistered children’s care home pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to two charges of committing carnal intercourse against the order of nature against a 15-year-old boy, three years ago. The offence carries up to 20 years’ imprisonment and caning upon conviction. “Previously, my client was charged at the Ampang Sessions Court and his passport was surrendered to that court for a different offence,” she said.

items.title: NPE's Kuchai Link closed as heavy rain triggers flash floods, traffic gridlock reported
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy downpour in the Klang Valley this afternoon has triggered flash floods around the city. New Pantai Expressway (NPE) highway operator in a posting on X said the inclement weather had forced a full closure along the Kuchai Link stretch from Sungai Besi towards Jalan Klang Lama. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Four Negri Sembilan BN-held exco posts remain vacant for now, says MB
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The four Negri Sembilan executive committee positions that became vacant following the move by Barisan Nasional assemblymen to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will remain vacant for now. Aminuddin said the state government has yet to make a decision on the status of the vacant posts. He said for the time being, the six remaining state executive councillors will oversee the portfolios previously held by the four Umno assemblymen.

items.title: Human-to-human hantavirus strain confirmed in cruise passenger: S. Africa
items.summary: JOHANNESBURG: The Andes strain of the hantavirus that is transmissible between humans has been confirmed in a passenger evacuated to South Africa from a stricken cruise ship, the health minister said Wednesday. Three people from the cruise ship have died, one of them in Johannesburg after she was transferred on a commercial flight from the Atlantic island of Saint Helena. "The preliminary tests show that, indeed, this is the Andes strain," South Africa's health minister Aaron Motsoaledi told a parliament committee.

items.title: Rafizi: MACC probe focuses on procedure, not money flows
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said investigations into him have so far focused on procedural matters rather than financial transactions regarding the RM1.1 billion semiconductor deal between the Economy Ministry and UK-based firm Arm Holdings. Speaking to reporters outside the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today, he said no questions during his statement recording touched on money flows or payments, contrary to earlier claims. "There were no questions on financial transactions.

items.title: Perak police arrest seven men in Teluk Intan fatal robbery, cable theft probe
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, May 6 — Police have arrested seven local men believed to be involved in a fatal robbery and cable theft cases around Teluk Intan. Hilir Perak district police chief ACP Chua Kok Lian said all the suspects, aged between 20 and 30, were arrested around Teluk Intan and in Bentong, Pahang. He said that three of the seven suspects arrested were believed to be involved in housebreaking cases and six cable theft incidents around Teluk Intan, resulting in losses estimated at RM200,000.

items.title: Suspect in assault of pregnant wife expected to be charged tomorrow
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A man who assaulted his wife until she miscarried last month will be charged at the Sungai Petani Court tomorrow. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan confirmed the matter today. Rosmaini Abd Raof, 43, will be charged under Section 326 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt with dangerous weapons or means.

items.title: AI and the strategic importance of information resilience
items.summary: ARTIFICIAL intelligence (AI) is changing the way information is generated, disseminated and consumed. In the contemporary digital environment, information has become a central arena of strategic competition. A state may have strong institutions, a resilient economy and capable security forces; yet it can still be weakened if public trust erodes, official information is challenged, or people become vulnerable to hostile information campaigns.

items.title: NGO lodges report against Negri Sembilan Umno chief over remarks on state ruler
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A non-governmental organisation is calling on police to investigate statements made by Negri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias for allegedly touching on the sensitivities and authority of the state's Yang di-Pertuan Besar. The police report was lodged by the chairman of the Taat Setia Sokongan Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Secretariat, Datuk Seri Megat D Shahriman Zaharudin, for the statement allegedly said on May 2. Last week, 14 Umno assemblymen withdrew their confidence from Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.

items.title: Sabah to leverage on advanced tech to strengthen security
items.summary: LAHAD DATU: Sabah will enhance security capabilities through the use of advanced technology, including artificial intelligence to ensure stability as the foundation of the state's economic growth. "In line with the Hala Tuju Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) 2.0 framework, we want security agencies to strengthen the use of AI in operations so that our security system is not only physically strong, but also digitally resilient. "Without strong security guarantees, investor confidence will be affected, economic activities will be hampered, and the well-being of the people will also suffer," he said in a statement after a security inspection in the Eastern Sabah Security Zone (EssZone) at the Dewan Perdana here.

items.title: Muda calls on Johor reps to oppose Bill on appointment of five unelected assemblymen
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 6 — Muda has urged Johor assemblymen who oppose the proposed appointment of five unelected assemblymen to raise their objections when the matter is brought to the state assembly tomorrow. Muda president Amira Aisya Abd Aziz said this was because details relating to the five appointed assemblymen were still unclear and lacked definition. She said any decision on the proposed Bill should take into account the views of all stakeholders, including all assemblymen.

items.title: Negri Sembilan Umno stays in state government, maintains no-confidence in Aminuddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno will remain in the state unity government but will not withdraw its no-confidence stance against Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. State Umno secretary Datuk Mustapha Nagoor said all 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen will continue to carry out their duties in the state government following a decision reached during the Umno-Pakatan Harapan Leadership Consultative Council meeting last night. "We respect the decision of the leadership to maintain cooperation with the Negri Sembilan unity government, as decided by the Umno Political Bureau and the Umno-PH Leadership Consultative Council.

items.title: Johor to trial Jalan Skudai contraflow lane ahead of RTS Link launch in 2027
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 5 — A contraflow lane will be implemented along the Senai-Johor Bahru corridor, particularly Jalan Skudai, to address critical traffic congestion following the commencement of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link operations expected next year. Mohamad Fazli said a data-driven approach had been adopted in assessing preparedness for the traffic flow increase following the RTS Link operations, including through the “Greater Johor Bahru & JS-SEZ Traffic Dispersal and Congestion Action Plan” study conducted via the Johor Public Transport Corporation (PAJ). He said the study identified 77 congestion hotspots grouped into 41 clusters, with 24 clusters categorised as serious in Johor Bahru, Pasir Gudang, Iskandar Puteri and Kulai.

items.title: Rural Sabah teacher earns global Cambridge recognition for inclusive learning efforts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian teacher has been named a regional winner of the 2026 Cambridge Dedicated Teacher Awards for advancing inclusive education for neurodivergent learners in rural Sabah. Velerie oversees individual education plans, mentors educators and works closely with families to support neurodivergent students. Cambridge International Education group managing director Rod Smith said Velerie's work highlighted the value of inclusive learning.

items.title: Utility issues and logistical challenges slow Sabah Pan Borneo Highway works
items.summary: KINABATANGAN: Several major constraints have been affecting Pan Borneo Highway projects in Sabah, said Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. He said the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway Project Phase 1B is among the government's strategic initiatives to strengthen the road infrastructure network, thereby boosting economic growth and connecting key areas on Sabah's east coast. The Pan Borneo Highway Sabah project was launched on April 24, 2016.

items.title: Negri Sembilan government no longer minority administration, says Aminuddin
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Negri Sembilan unity government is no longer a minority government, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said. The Sikamat assemblyman said this follows the consensus reached during the Umno-Pakatan Harapan Leadership Consultative Council meeting last night, in which both Umno and PH agreed to maintain cooperation in the Negri Sembilan unity government. Aminuddin, who is also PKR vice-president, said the state government has now returned to its original status, effectively resolving the recent leadership and political crisis.

items.title: Contraflow lane to start on Senai-JB corridor ahead of RTS Link ops
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: A contraflow lane will be implemented along the Senai-Johor Baru corridor, particularly Jalan Skudai, to address critical traffic congestion following the commencement of the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System Link operations expected next year. Fazli said a data-driven approach had been adopted in assessing preparedness for the traffic flow increase following the RTS Link operations, including through the "Greater Johor Baru & JS-SEZ Traffic Dispersal and Congestion Action Plan" study conducted via the Johor Public Transport Corporation (PAJ). He said the study identified 77 congestion hotspots grouped into 41 clusters, with 24 clusters categorised as serious in Johor Bahru, Pasir Gudang, Iskandar Puteri and Kulai.

items.title: Court dismisses Ratu Naga's bid to refer questions of law to Federal Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today dismissed Syarul Ema Rena Abu Samah's bid to refer two questions of law to the Federal Court over being denied legal representation during questioning by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). Therefore, the court ruled that Syarul Ema Rena's application did not meet the threshold under Section 84 of the Courts of Judicature Act 1964. Today's proceedings were attended by lawyer Latheefa Koya representing Syarul Ema Rena, while senior federal counsel Nur Irmawatie Daud represented the Attorney General.

items.title: Gombak welfare home operator pleads not guilty to carnal intercourse charges against minor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The operator of an unlicensed children's welfare home in Gombak was hauled to court for the third time today to face two charges of committing carnal intercourse against the nature of order on a 15-year-old boy three years ago. The fresh charges at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court bring the total he faces to 11, following four counts of physical and non-physical sexual abuse at the Ampang Sessions Court. At the Selayang Sessions Court earlier, he pleaded not guilty to four charges of abusing four children under his care and another count for operating the unregistered home.

items.title: Teen charged with dangerous driving causing cyclist's death
items.summary: KUCHING: A teenage boy pleaded not guilty in the magistrate's court here today to a charge of dangerous driving causing the death of a cyclist on May 1. He was accused of driving dangerously and colliding with a cyclist, causing death, at Jalan Stutong here at 5.30am on May 1. In addition, a conviction would also lead to disqualification from holding a driving licence for a period of not less than five years and cancellation of the licence if holding a probationary driving licence.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain warning for nine states, KL and Putrajaya until 5pm
items.summary: In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas include Kedah, Perak, Penang, Selangor, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, Johor, Sarawak and Sabah. In Selangor, the affected areas are Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang. The warning also covers Melaka.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM orders coordinated action on unregulated houses of worship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed several ministries and agencies to coordinate efforts to address issues involving unregulated houses of worship. He said the discussions were aimed at finding solutions to issues involving unregulated houses of worship nationwide. On Feb 9, Anwar said the government would not allow the construction of unregulated houses of worship.

items.title: MCMC calls for public support after 10 ICT projects shortlisted for WSIS Prizes 2026
items.summary: Communications & Infrastructure Disaster Recovery (CIDR) Plan (Category AL C5 — Building confidence and security in ICT use). Human-Centred Digital Governance Through MCMC’s Five-Year Plan 2030 (Category AL C6 — Enabling environment). Sebenarnya.my portal (Category AL C9 — Media).

items.title: Seberang Perai council reviewing rentals for 5,000 stalls after PM’s directive to ease traders’ burden
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, May 6 — The Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) is reviewing rental rates for traders in Seberang Perai following Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s directive yesterday to lower business premises rental. Mayor Datuk Baderul Amin Abdul Hamid said the matter requires careful assessment before any decision on the rate reduction is made. He said MBSP welcomes the proposal as it could help ease the burden on traders, especially amid current economic challenges.

items.title: Absence of money trail does not weaken Lim Guan Eng corruption case, court told
items.summary: “However, this did not affect my investigation, as it is supported by the testimony of the key witness, Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli, a former director of Consortium Zenith Construction Sdn Bhd (CZCSB). My investigation also relies on documents I obtained, which indicate that meetings were held inside a car involving Zarul Ahmad, Gnanaraja and the accused. Zulhilmi: I considered Gnanaraja’s statement regarding the RM2 million payment to the accused, which is supported by Zarul Ahmad’s testimony.

items.title: Labuan MAIWP engineer, two company owners remanded in RM2.6m contract case
items.summary: LABUAN, May 6 — A senior assistant engineer at the Labuan Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), and two company owners, have been remanded for six days to assist investigations into an alleged abuse of power involving a facilities management and maintenance contract worth about RM2.6 million. Magistrate Dr Melissa Chia issued the remand order effective from today until May 12, following an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). The senior assistant engineer is alleged to have misused his position by using the licence of a main contractor to appoint subcontractor companies linked to his family members to carry out facilities management and maintenance works at Kompleks Darul Kifayah MAIWP here.

items.title: Van driver remanded three days over death of Year Two pupil in Batu Pahat
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, May 6 — A 60-year-old van driver has been remanded for three days starting today to assist police in the probe into the death of a Year Two pupil from a primary school in Parit Sulong here. Batu Pahat police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani said the remand application for the man was made to assist investigations under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act (APJ) 1987. “The remand order on the suspect was issued by Magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman from today to May 8, after he was brought to the Batu Pahat Magistrate’s Court earlier this morning,” he said when contacted by the media today.

items.title: Azam Baki: MACC recommends charges against two in Arm Holdings chip probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recommended two people involved in the chip patent deal with UK semiconductor giant Arm Holdings to be charged, its outgoing chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said today. “Two people. Malaysia secured 32 Arm design licences — 25 basic and seven advanced.

items.title: MACC targets recovery of RM8b in remaining 1MDB assets, nearly 75pc already reclaimed
items.summary: “In all, I think there’s about US$2 billion (RM7.9 billion) still to be recovered,” Azam told a press conference here. The asset recovery efforts began in 2019, with RM1.155 billion retrieved that year, followed by RM14.257 billion in 2020 and RM5.117 billion in 2021. “Subsequently, RM400.38 million was recovered in 2022, RM8.088 billion in 2023, RM715.95 million in 2024 and RM1.566 billion in 2025,” Azam said.

items.title: MACC detains eight in Padini-linked probe, statements recorded from 10
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained eight individuals to assist in investigations linked to the freezing of several bank accounts belonging to garment retailer Padini Holdings Bhd, and its subsidiaries. MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the probe is ongoing, with statements recorded from 10 individuals so far, while another 15 are being traced to assist investigations. The company also said it had engaged external legal counsel and taken necessary steps, including applying for relief to unfreeze the accounts.

items.title: Johor to form school safety task force after Year Two pupil killed by van in Batu Pahat
items.summary: Johor education and information committee chairman Aznan Tamin said the initiative was aimed at investigating and improving students’ safety inside and outside school premises. He said this also included school-based activities. On Nur Aini Umairah’s death, Aznan said the Johor Education Department is expected to provide counselling assistance to the victim’s family.

items.title: Deeper Malaysia–Philippines partnership crucial to shaping Asean’s future, says former Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Penang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Malaysia can play a vital role in shaping Asean’s future by working closely with the Philippines, the current Asean chair, to advance key regional initiatives, said former Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Penang Tun Ahmad Fuzi Abdul Razak. He said that among the proposed regional initiatives are the Digital Asean Commons, aimed at integrating digital economies, data governance and innovation ecosystems across the region, as well as the Regional Supply Chain Resilience Initiative, which aims to position Asean as the world’s most reliable and diversified manufacturing hub. “Other initiatives include Asean Green Transition Partnership, which aims to mobilise public and private sector investment in sustainable energy and climate resilience, and the Asean Talent Mobility Framework to facilitate the movement of skilled professionals across borders,” he said in his keynote address at the Asean Leadership and Partnership Forum (ALPF) 2026 here today.

items.title: Palestinian ambassador hails Malaysia’s support, urges deeper role in post‑war rebuilding and trade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Palestine’s Ambassador to Malaysia Jehad Fouad Alqdra has praised Malaysia as one of Palestine’s most consistent and principled international supporters in its enduring struggle for statehood and self-determination. Palestine’s Ambassador to Malaysia Jehad Fouad Alqdra during an interview with Bernama at his office recently. The ambassador also invited Malaysia to participate in post-war reconstruction efforts in Gaza.

items.title: PBAPP to lay two main pipelines under Penang water contingency plan to raise supply by 2030
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 6 — The Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) will install two 1.8-metre (m) diameter main pipelines to channel more treated water from the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant (WTP) to the Barat Daya district on the island as part of efforts to strengthen water supply by 2030. PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the project is a key component of Phase 3 of the Water Contingency Plan 2030 (WCP 2030), with an estimated total cost of about RM416 million involving pipe procurement, installation works, excavation and road reinstatement works, as well as land acquisition costs. He said the first pipeline will connect the Sungai Dua WTP to the connection point of the Penang Third Undersea Twin Pipeline Project (3rd PTSP) in Butterworth, involving a route of about 13 kilometres on the mainland.

items.title: Malaysia’s 1MDB recovery hits RM31.3b as of 2025, ranks among world’s highest, says MACC chief
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — Malaysia has successfully recovered RM31.3 billion, or 74.5 per cent, of the RM42 billion misappropriated from 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) funds as of last year, according to Tan Sri Azam Baki. Azam said that 1MDB asset recovery efforts began in 2019, with RM1.155 billion retrieved that year, followed by RM14.257 billion in 2020 and RM5.117 billion in 2021. “Subsequently, RM400.38 million was recovered in 2022, RM8.088 billion in 2023, RM715.95 million in 2024 and RM1.566 billion in 2025,” he said.

items.title: Johor draws RM10b in potential investments after Shanghai trip, says exco
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 6 — Johor has attracted strong interest from 36 international investors, with potential initial investment commitments worth about RM10 billion, state investment, trade, consumer affairs and human resources committee chairman Lee Ting Han said today. Speaking during the question-and-answer session at the Johor state legislative assembly, Lee said the investment potential followed the Johor government delegation’s recent working visit to Shanghai, China. Lee said the delegation also held comprehensive discussions with several major industry players and their supplier networks to strengthen Johor’s investment potential and supply chain development.

items.title: Police nab civil servant in Tawau break‑in case; recover laptops, bags
items.summary: District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said the suspect was detained at about 10.45am by a team from the Criminal Investigation Department of the Tawau district police headquarters. Police said several items were reported missing, including two laptops, a 60-inch television, a wristwatch, several pairs of shoes, handbags and other personal belongings. Jasmin said police take house break-in cases seriously and urged the public to strengthen home security and report any suspicious activity immediately.

items.title: Tabung Haji system ensures Haj eligibility based on quota and queue, not higher payments, says licensed operator
items.summary: MECCA, May 6 — The queue system and quota allocation are the main criteria used by Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) to determine Malaysian pilgrims’ eligibility, with no influence from financial capacity or higher package payments. Licensed Haj operators (PJH) operations director Anwar Ahmad said all prospective pilgrims, including those applying through PJH packages, are subject to the same processes and criteria set by TH. “Ensure that every Haj offer comes from TH and only deal with licensed Haj operators to avoid the risk of fraud,” he said.
